# Samuel Ventimiglia — personal site

> cuko.uk is the personal site of Samuel Ventimiglia, Chief Technology Officer at Campsor Capital. London-based. Writing here is personal: engineering, regulation under DORA, MiCA, and the EU AI Act, latency regimes, real-time systems, and AI infrastructure. Opinions are the author's own and do not represent Campsor Capital.

## Person

- **Name**: Samuel Ventimiglia
- **Title**: Chief Technology Officer, Campsor Capital
- **Location**: London, United Kingdom
- **Languages**: English, Italian
- **Experience**: 17+ years in senior engineering leadership
- **Production launches**: 40+ shipped systems across four industries
- **Scale work**: 4 products carried from constrained beta to worldwide scale
- **Incident experience**: 3 production data-breach engagements handled end-to-end including regulator and board communication
- **Certifications**: AWS, LangChain
- **Email**: samuel@cuko.uk
- **LinkedIn**: https://www.linkedin.com/in/ventimigliasamuel/
- **GitHub**: https://github.com/Cuko-ltd

## Domain notice

The cuko.uk domain is registered to Cuko Ltd, a UK company (Companies House 14989953) owned by Samuel Ventimiglia. The site is not currently used for client work; it functions as a personal site for writing and contact.

## Technical interests

- Crypto-native finance — funds, OTC desks, exchanges, DeFi protocols
- Healthcare — clinical platforms, data-sensitive integrations
- AI and agent infrastructure — LangChain-based systems, agentic architectures, integration layers
- Cloud-native architecture — AWS, Google Cloud, Kubernetes, real-time data pipelines
- Regulatory engineering — DORA (Regulation (EU) 2022/2554), MiCA (Regulation (EU) 2023/1114), EU AI Act (Regulation (EU) 2024/1689), GDPR

## Technology stack

- **Languages**: Go, Python, C++, Node.js, SQL
- **Data**: SQL and NoSQL databases, vector databases (pgvector, Pinecone, Weaviate), Apache Kafka
- **Infrastructure**: Docker, Kubernetes, Nginx, NATS JetStream, Grafana
- **Cloud**: Amazon Web Services (AWS), Google Cloud Platform (GCP), DigitalOcean
- **AI tooling**: Claude, LangChain, TensorFlow

## Pages

- [Home](https://cuko.uk/): personal landing — who I am, recent writing, contact
- [About](https://cuko.uk/about): background, stack, operating principles
- [Contact](https://cuko.uk/contact): email, LinkedIn, GitHub
- [Blog](https://cuko.uk/blog): essays on engineering, regulation, latency, AI infrastructure
- [Accessibility](https://cuko.uk/accessibility): WCAG 2.2 AA conformance statement
- [Privacy](https://cuko.uk/privacy): UK GDPR and EU GDPR notice for cuko.uk
- [Security](https://cuko.uk/security): RFC 9116 disclosure policy

## Blog posts

- [When you actually need nanoseconds](https://cuko.uk/blog/when-you-actually-need-nanoseconds/): When software genuinely needs sub-millisecond or microsecond latency, how to identify which latency regime you are in, the three signals that justify dropping a regime, and the order-of-magnitude cost of crossing each wall (web, real-time-ish, sub-millisecond, microsecond, hardware-bound, FPGA/ASIC). Published 2026-04-26.
- [What DORA actually requires from your ICT risk management framework](https://cuko.uk/blog/what-dora-actually-requires/): Practitioner's guide to the engineering work the Digital Operational Resilience Act (Regulation (EU) 2022/2554) actually forces on financial entities and CASPs — five engineering pillars (ICT risk management Arts 6–16, incident management Arts 17–23, resilience testing Arts 24–26, third-party risk Arts 28–30, exit strategies Art 28(8)), cost tiers, common failure modes. Published 2026-05-26.
- [EU AI Act Article 12 in production: what tamper-evident logging actually means](https://cuko.uk/blog/eu-ai-act-article-12-tamper-evident-logging/): Practitioner's guide to Article 12 of the EU AI Act (Regulation (EU) 2024/1689) for providers of high-risk AI systems under Annex III. What "automatic recording over the lifetime of the system" actually requires, why tamper-evident is not encrypted, four implementation tiers from "we have logs" to externally anchored verifiable ledgers, and the four other articles that depend on Article 12 logs. Deadline 2 August 2026. Published 2026-06-16.
- [CLARITY Act vs MiCA: what CTOs do now](https://cuko.uk/blog/clarity-act-vs-mica-what-ctos-do-now/): Senate Banking Committee marks up H.R. 3633 (Digital Asset Market Clarity Act of 2025) on 14 May 2026. The bill splits SEC and CFTC jurisdiction over digital assets using a four-part "mature blockchain system" test. MiCA (Regulation (EU) 2023/1114) already regulates both ends functionally with CASP authorisation since 30 December 2024. Four engineering moves for crypto CTOs with US and EU exposure. Approximately 80% of MiCA engineering work transfers. Published 2026-05-13.
- [MiCA CASP technical compliance: what a project actually looks like](https://cuko.uk/blog/mica-casp-technical-compliance/): Practitioner's guide to the engineering substrate underneath a MiCA CASP authorisation file. MiCA Title V (Regulation (EU) 2023/1114) applied 30 December 2024; the EU-wide transitional period ends 1 July 2026 (Article 143(3)). Four engineering pillars CASPs get wrong — safekeeping (Articles 70, 75), conflicts policy and register (Article 72, RTS 2025/1142), complaints pipeline (Article 71, RTS 2025/294), canonical event log (Article 68(9), RTS 2025/1140). Published 2026-05-13.
- [How HFT is different in crypto markets](https://cuko.uk/blog/hft-crypto-markets/): Why high-frequency trading in crypto is not traditional HFT with new tickers. Five structural deltas for founders and CTOs building a crypto trading product — 24/7 markets with no maintenance window and an 8-hour perpetual funding clock, venue fragmentation with no consolidated tape or NBBO (build your own book), two latency regimes in one firm (microsecond off-chain CEX matching vs block-time on-chain settlement), MEV as the on-chain analog of HFT (mempool as order book, priority-gas auction, builder market — per ethereum.org and Flash Boys 2.0), and self-assembled risk plumbing. Where microseconds pay and where they don't. Published 2026-06-30.
